public String largestMerge(String word1, String word2) {

    StringBuilder merge = new StringBuilder();

    int i = 0, j = 0;

    while (i < word1.length() && j < word2.length()) {

        int len1 = word1.length() - i;

        int len2 = word2.length() - j;

        int minLen = Math.min(len1, len2);

        int k = 0;

        int compare = 0;

        while (k < minLen) {

            char c1 = word1.charAt(i + k);

            char c2 = word2.charAt(j + k);

            if (c1 != c2) {

                compare = c1 - c2;

                break;

            }

            k++;

        }

        if (compare == 0) {

            compare = len1 - len2;

        }

        if (compare >= 0) {

            merge.append(word1.charAt(i));

            i++;

        } else {

            merge.append(word2.charAt(j));

            j++;

        }

    }

    // append remaining of word1 or word2

    while (i < word1.length()) {

        merge.append(word1.charAt(i));

        i++;

    }

    while (j < word2.length()) {

        merge.append(word2.charAt(j));

        j++;

    }

    return merge.toString();

}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String largestMerge(String word1, String word2) {
        StringBuilder merge = new StringBuilder();
        int i = 0, j = 0;
        while (i < word1.length() && j < word2.length()) {
            int len1 = word1.length() - i;
            int len2 = word2.length() - j;
            int minLen = Math.min(len1, len2);
            int k = 0;
            int compare = 0;
            while (k < minLen) {
                char c1 = word1.charAt(i + k);
                char c2 = word2.charAt(j + k);
                if (c1 != c2) {
                    compare = c1 - c2;
                    break;
                }
                k++;
            }
            if (compare == 0) {
                compare = len1 - len2;
            }
            if (compare >= 0) {
                merge.append(word1.charAt(i));
                i++;
            } else {
                merge.append(word2.charAt(j));
                j++;
            }
        }
        while (i < word1.length()) {
            merge.append(word1.charAt(i));
            i++;
        }
        while (j < word2.length()) {
            merge.append(word2.charAt(j));
            j++;
        }
        return merge.toString();
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O((N + M)^2)
space complexity: O(N + M)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm greedily selects the next character from the string whose remaining substring is lexicographically larger. It avoids creating substrings by comparing characters directly, which reduces memory usage. The time complexity is quadratic in the worst case due to character-by-character comparisons, but this is efficient enough for the problem constraints. The approach ensures optimal lexicographical order but may have higher time complexity for cases requiring full substring comparisons.
